title: Evaluation and Docs Roadmap Follow-through
goal: Implement the non-deferred evaluation and documentation roadmap issues: #729, #730, #731, and #732.
Run the affected model-CLI evaluation scenarios and convert concrete friction into package or documentation fixes.
Compress repeated documentation without flattening the abstraction ladder.
non_goals: Do not implement deferred issues #595, #627, or #661.
Do not relocate generated Python adapters under generated/python in this slice.
Do not do broad large-file remediation or unrelated adaptive-assurance dogfooding.

intent.outcome: The active evaluation tracks have current probes/fixes, documentation repetition is reduced, and deferred issues remain explicitly out of scope.
intent.constraints: Use checked-in planning as execution authority; keep tracker issues as intake references; preserve compact CLI-first workflow surfaces.
intent.latitude: Tune model-harness scenarios, compact workflow/help/docs surfaces, and documentation structure where evidence shows agent confusion or repeated prose.
intent.escalation: Escalate before changing generated CLI layout, broad file architecture, external CI policy, or deferred issue scope.

execution.next_step: Archive this execplan, close GitHub #729-#732, commit, and push.
execution.proof: Harness scenario runs for #730/#731/#732 where available plus docs/package checks and make check before closeout.

invariants: Planning state remains schema-valid and summary-visible.
Evaluation scenarios stay adaptive dogfooding aids, not formal pass/fail benchmarks.
Docs preserve a navigable abstraction ladder with local summaries and canonical detail owners.

intent_continuity.larger intended outcome: Agents should have obvious, compact next steps for broad-work preparation, Memory routing, planning artifact integrity, and documentation navigation.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: More precise docs ownership, focused evaluation evidence for Memory/broad-work/planning integrity, and sharper model-harness scoring for observed Copilot failure modes.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: Planning artifact integrity failures can appear as confident false-valid path claims or nonexistent lifecycle commands, not just fixture mutation.
iterative_follow_through.proof achieved now: Focused harness tests, full pytest suite, ruff, maintainer surfaces, planning doctor, planning surface check, and make check passed.

intent_interpretation.literal request: Implement the rest after deferring #595, #627, and #661.

execution_bounds.stop before touching: Deferred issues #595/#627/#661, generated/python relocation, unrelated large-file cleanup, or external CI/review-template policy.

execution_run.what happened: Implemented #729 by adding a canonical documentation-owner map and compressing README learn-more repetition; implemented #730/#732 by tightening model CLI harness scoring for final-answer-only forbidden phrases, unsupported planning promotion commands, and false valid-path claims; implemented #731 by running Memory routing and capture probes and recording evidence.

execution_run.changed surfaces: scripts/model_cli_harness/run_model_cli_harness.py; tests/test_model_cli_harness.py; tools/model-cli-harness/model-task-weakness-ledger.json; README.md; docs/index.md; docs/documentation-status.md; planning state.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
signals found: Harness false-positive: forbidden response phrases were detected from echoed workflow text rather than the final answer.
Harness false-negative: planning artifact integrity accepted unsupported `promote-lane` recommendations and false valid-path claims.
signals fixed: Final-answer-only forbidden phrase scoring for response phrases.
Semantic warnings for unsupported `agentic-planning-bootstrap promote-lane` recommendations.
Semantic warnings for missing execplan refs when the agent claims referenced planning paths are valid.
signals dismissed: Copilot postmortem feedback was noisy in one run; not routed as a package issue because the workflow already treats model feedback as advisory evidence.
